mapper.select: select id as userId, email_address, password, family_name, first_name, employee_number, is_admin, is_deleted as userIsDeleted, register_date_time as userRegisterDateTime, update_date_time as userUpdateDateTime, concat(family_name,first_name) as fullName from m_user where email_address = #{emailAddress} and is_deleted != 1

select id as userId, email_address, password, family_name, first_name, employee_number, is_admin, is_deleted as userIsDeleted, register_date_time as userRegisterDateTime, update_date_time as userUpdateDateTime from m_user where is_deleted != 1 order by employee_number asc

select id as userId, email_address, password, family_name, first_name, employee_number, is_admin, is_deleted as userIsDeleted, register_date_time as userRegisterDateTime, update_date_time as userUpdateDateTime from m_user where is_deleted != 1 and( family_name like '%' || #{search} || '%' or first_name like '%' || #{search} || '%' or employee_number like '%' || #{search} || '%'
or is_admin = '1'
or is_admin = '0'
) order by employee_number asc

select id as userId, email_address, password, family_name, first_name, employee_number, is_admin, is_deleted as userIsDeleted, register_date_time as userRegisterDateTime, update_date_time as userUpdateDateTime from m_user where employee_number = #{employeeNumber}
mapper.insert: insert into m_user ( email_address, password, family_name, first_name, employee_number, is_admin ) values ( #{emailAddress}, #{password}, #{familyName}, #{firstName}, #{employeeNumber}, #{isAdmin} )
mapper.select: select id as userId, email_address, password, family_name, first_name, employee_number, is_admin, is_deleted as userIsDeleted, register_date_time as userRegisterDateTime, update_date_time as userUpdateDateTime from m_user where id = #{userId} and is_deleted != 1
mapper.update:
update m_user set email_address = #{emailAddress}, family_name = #{familyName}, first_name = #{firstName}, employee_number = #{employeeNumber}, is_admin = #{isAdmin}
where id = #{userId}
